entry.title: Re: Adopting General Display Name
entry.content:
Hi @HarveyXu,

thank you very much for your article.

I'm looking for a solution to display the preferred Name (when edited from customer) in the result of People Search. Is General Display Name going to update not only the name in the People Profile but also the result in the search bar when someone is looking for that person using the preferred name?

Best regards

entry.title: Move data only to first product in the hierarchy below using data action in SAC
entry.content:
I have a Product dimension which has 2 hierarchies, PL6 and PL7. The hierarchy is something like this. I want to move the data from hierarchy PL6 level to PL7 but only to the first product in PL7 hierarchy. I'm trying to do it with the below code using FOREACH. It is moving the data to all the products in the PL7. [p/SKUPL6] is the attribute which has the connection between PL7 AND PL6 hierarchies. Each product at PL7 level has this attribute SKUPL6 which has the value for PL6 under which it belongs to. I'm trying to use FOREACH to move data from PL6 for each iteration to PL7 and then delete the product if SKUPL6 = null.. As SKUPL6 only has values for PL7 products and for PL6 products this attribute is blank. The below code is copying data for all products below PL6 but not deleting PL6 value within the FOREACH. I'm trying to delete the PL6 value so it only copies the value for the first product and for the next product it will have no value in PL6 to copy below.

How can I achieve this?

entry.title: Assistance on SAP Cloud ALM monitoring setup for API calls to/from managed sytsem/service
entry.content:
Hi,

We want configure SAP Cloud ALM to monitor API calls (like ODATA, SOAP, Rest APIs) which are coming into and going out of our SAP S/4HANA system.

Is it possible to configure monitoring for a system's API calls in SAP Cloud ALM?

If so could you please provide the respective help documents?

 

Thanks & Regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Assistance on SAP Cloud ALM monitoring setup for API...
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ALM for Operations provides several "use cases" for different monitoring scenarios. These include "Health Monitoring", "Integration & Exception Monitoring", and so on.

A full list of supported monitoring content (metrics) can be found by clicking the "content" pages for a use case on the SAP Cloud ALM for Operations Expert Portal.

Currently SAP Cloud ALM for Operations does not offer monitoring content (metrics) related to the collection of data about API calls made or received by a service/system.

 

If you wish to influence the future development of SAP Cloud ALM and provide direction to our Developers in relation to this topic, please use the options outlined in this KBA:
3391676 - SAP Continuous Influence for SAP Cloud ALM

entry.title: Re: New Community Package: A Cloud Integration Alternative for SAP Process Orchestration Adapter Mod
entry.content: Informative blog . Thanks for sharing .

entry.title: Re: Unlock New Horizons in Automation by Upgrading to SAP Build Process Automation Agent 3

Informative blog . Thanks for sharing . 

Regards ,

entry.title: Withholding tax not calculated in a SD invoice
entry.content:
WITHOLDING TAX NOT INTEGRATED BETWEEN FI & SD at S/4 HANA

 

 

We have configured the Extended Withholding tax calculation for Spain in the SPRO with a completely set for FI and SD 

 

The TWES is a standard condition price which has been use for the price in the SD orders

 

 

 

When we try to post a new order the withholding condition type is detected in the order but is not calculated in the final invoice in the SD module.

 

SD ORDERSD INVOICE

The   invoice don´t  has the corresponding withholding line …only VAT is calculated

 

FI-AR  FB70 invoice

On the opposite side we can calculate a FI-AR invoice by using the TCODE FB70 with the withholding tax froM the same customer configured in the master data 

The FB70 triggers a pop up telling that withholding tax is mandatory for this customer, we enter the base amount  in the WHT tag and the withholding tax calcultio line apperars in the FI document.

 

 

The final posting document with the WHT calculation

Mahmoud Samy

entry.title: Re: MRP X MRP live
entry.content:
Hello ADILSONBENNY,

The key difference between MRP in ECC and MRP Live in S/4HANA is processing speed. MRP in ECC uses a row-wise database search, while MRP Live in S/4HANA searches both row-wise and column-wise, significantly enhancing performance. Additionally, when MRP runs in ECC, it can slow down other transactional processes, leading to delays for end users. In contrast, MRP Live in S/4HANA mitigates this issue with its more efficient row-and-column-based search, which is pushed down to the database level, whereas MRP in ECC searches for objects at the application level.

entry.title: How to manage document restriction in SAP public cloud? Without extension in BTP
entry.content:
Hi experts, how can I restrict access to specific documents?

For our client, we are looking for a solution to restrict access to specific documents. I found some blogs about extensibility in BTP, but we would like to use basic from the public cloud before extension.

many thanks for your advices

Thanks very much for your help,

 

Dana

entry.title: upgrading SAP BW on non-HANA database but S/4 as source
entry.content:
Hi All,

Upgrading from SAP BW 7.0 on oracle to SAP BW 7.5 on oracle (ECC6 as a source). Larger plan not decided yet but may stick with BW 7.5 but use S/4 HANA as a source in future.

1. Is it possible to upgrade to BW 7.5 on sql with ECC6 as a main source of datasources? This question also related to the fact for

   - a purely technical upgrade , we don't convert to SAP BW 7.5 infoproviders like composite providers , ADSOs etc. We upgrade the system but leave the objects as is but in sap BW 7.5 (old objects supported in 7.5?). But if we do this , would we be able to interface with S/4 HANA as a datasource later? Will we be ready for a BW bridge for Datasphere?

2. OR is it better that we do a functional upgrade, convert 7.0 objects to 7.5 objects (ADSOs , composite providers, open hub etc.) 

Regards,

Cleo

entry.title: Re: New Member intro - Emmanuel Katto
entry.content:
Hello Uganda!

Nice to meet you Emmanuel Katto

entry.title: Re: Share content across multiple sites
entry.content:
Hello,
To share content across multiple sites while maintaining control from the master catalog, you can use a “sharing by reference” approach. This allows you to edit and sync components in the master catalog (contentCatalogEU) without removing them from the sub catalog (contentCatalogFR). Ensure that the shared components are referenced correctly in both catalogs to maintain consistency. floridablue

Best Regards,
Julia Goff

entry.title: Detailed steps to establish integration between Azure blob Storage & BTP CPI
entry.content:
Hi All,

In this blog, you would be able to have a detailed look on below points.

1. Azure storage creation on trial account & setup

2. Able to post a file to the azure storage created on step#1 using CPI IFLOW

Let's dive into the technical aspects:

Below are steps needed to setup at the azure end:

  1. Create a free trial for Azure portal access on link.

If you don't have an Azure subscription, go to https://azure.microsoft.com/free/ to create a free account before you begin.

Overview shown below:

2. Create a Resource group after creating the subscription.

3. Create a storage account once the resource group is created.

4. Create a container (Blob storage) under the storage account created in the above step.

5. Get the container properties to get the BLOB path as shown below.

Blob path - 

https://rprohinistorage.blob.core.windows.net/rohinicontainer

6. Go to Azure Portal -> Security -> Access Keys 

Get the shared access key from view & use it as secure Parameter on the azure Storage adapter.

We have completed the setup needed on Azure portal for our usecase.

Now, let's move ahead with the steps needed on CPI end.

1.   Create an IFlow - HTTP to Azure Storage

2. Configure the details on HTTP sender channel

3. Configure the respective details on Azure receiver channel with the info we have from Azure portal account

 

TESTING:

Trigger the data to HTTPS endpoint from postman

Testing shows success logs & file created on the Azure blob container.

Let's confirm the file contents to validate if data matches from postman.

CPI logs:

 

Below are the details which I have created for my subscription to use: (This is currently deleted & i have just listed down details for reference purpose)

Azure BLOB Storage 

Subscription - Free Trial

Subscription ID - c8342d9c-4731-49fd-8926-042db82541ff

 Access key -  

J5v3PdB6e5QsFKvaTtF0eWlKHSNIBgIBqUMtlOtlyuaZhWPTIqCGgTqD3LUtBEjhiZfzHDwmCwG8+AStm0TgsQ==

Resource - Rohiniresource

Storage - rprohinistorage

Container - rohinicontainer

Secure Parameter from monitoring – AccessKeyRP

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Cheers!

Rohini Potham

entry.title: Re: PL by Segment
entry.content:
I found my mistake. I was using wrong field name. Correct one should be like-

/* SELECT FROM [dbo].[JDT1] */
DECLARE FDT TimeStamp;
FDT := /* JDT1."RefDate" */'[%0]';

/* SELECT FROM [dbo].[JDT1] */
DECLARE TDT TimeStamp;
TDT := /* JDT1."RefDate" */'[%1]';

Query is running well now. Sorry for the inconvenience.

entry.title: Re: Getting Started with Edge Integration Cell on AWS: A Setup Guide Using SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi ,

Thank you for the informative overview of the Edge Integration Cell (EIC) and its AWS deployment. The hybrid model it offers is ideal for balancing cloud flexibility with on-premise security. The step-by-step guide for both quick and high-availability setups is especially helpful for those migrating from SAP Process Integration.

A big thanks to the SAP team—Weikun Liu, Sangeetha Krishnamoorthy, Dante Alipio Jr., and Sivakumar N—for their valuable contributions. I’ll reach out to paa@sap.com if I have any questions.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: A Practical Guide to Naming iFlows in SAP Cloud Integration (SCI)
entry.content:
Hi ,

Thank you for the clear guide on naming iflows in SAP Cloud Integration. I agree that a structured naming convention is crucial for maintainability and support. The breakdown of your naming template and the examples provided are very helpful. Maintaining unique identifiers and a centralized knowledge base will enhance collaboration and prevent duplication. Consistency really does make a difference!

Regards ,

entry.title: How it was SAP Inside Track Porto 2024
entry.content:
Saturday, 28 SEP we had the first SAP Inside Track event in Porto.
In fact, it started on the 27th SET with an SAP CodeJam taught by Vitaly, which included a day of training, interspersed with coffee breaks in the morning and afternoon.
The event was organized by the following:
-Manuel Robalinho
-Arnaldo Calçada
-Pedro Rodrigues
-Pedro Marques
-Pedro Vieira
-Fatima Linhares
-Matheus Brazil
-Antonio Elias
 
As volunteers, we had the following colleagues:
-António Linhares Oliveira
-Simone Thami de Queiroz
-João Calçada
-Heyd Magnus
-Ernesto Martelo Junior
-Eloisa Silva
-Micael Mesquita
-Yvana Boufleuer
-Catarina Robalinho
-Carolina Albuquerque
-Luciana Caliman
 
The event was led by Arnaldo Calçada and Fátima Linhares. It was planned for 200 participants, and in terms of registrations, it was complete. 
It took place at The Lodge Hotel in Porto (located in Ribeira de Gaia), which proved very good, beautiful, and versatile for this type of event.
 
The event had coffee breaks in the morning and afternoon, and the event hotel offered lunch for organizers, volunteers, and sponsors.
 
At the reception for participants, there was a bag and an event T-shirt for everyone.
 
We had photographic coverage by a Volunteer (Kelzia Robalinho) and short interviews with participants to hear about the organization, lectures, and location. This allowed us to gather lessons learned and suggestions from various sources.
 
The topics and speakers of the lectures were:
0. SAP Communities and Knowledge Sharing on SAP Inside Track by Marco Santos
1. SAP Project: Technology or business? by Antonio Andrade
2. The era of Digitalization and Sustainability by Filipe Silva
3. Global eCompliance with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance by Felipe Borges
4. SAP Implementation Change Management how to manage organizations and people by Marta Mota
5. SAP Integration Suite - Security Guidelines - what can go wrong? by Antonio Vaz
6. SAP HANA multi-model examples by Rudnicki, Witalij
7. People, Process and Technology as successful enablers for excellence in industrial operations by Tiago Fernandes
8. Gen AI by Bruno Costa
9. SAP Build Work Zone Content Federation deep dive by Fabio Pagoti
10. AI capabilities and data security issues by Abílio Cardoso
 
At the end,Tuna Academica da FEUP we had a musical attraction by Tuna Académica from FEUP, who rocked with their brilliance!
 
The entire event was broadcast via YouTube, on the page https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=miFkEAnpLuk
 
Link to event fotos: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1gFas-vC-gQjsAkaSExQZyXrPNQC5b0o2?usp=sharing
 
Link to the PDF lectures: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1pCkMvXah4T-vpK1awKvzV2MILstYql_y?usp=drive_link

entry.title: Re: Data Access Control in SAP Datasphere - Hierarchy DAC
entry.content: @paddyw great article. Quick question: Are the access control table and DAC stored in the Space with the view? How can one safeguard these two so that other users of the space do not tamper or edit them, thereby compromising security?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Custom field in service entry sheet Fiori App

entry.title: Wave Creation is not possible with Activity area condition for template determination
entry.content:
The wave creation is not working when we select a condition field Activity area within a condition table. the system is not able the create a wave. 

The system is working when we test with only Warehouse number but when we add the Activity area at the level of condition table the system cannot create the wave. 

The rough bin determination is set at the level of the storage type and the warehouse process type but still not working.

entry.title: Re: Custom field in service entry sheet Fiori App
entry.content: @Daniel_NTT try then to extend directly the SEGW project like here: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_NETWEAVER_AS_ABAP_752/68bf513362174d54b58cddec28794093/c972141b977a4182930c192bfaf2c0b1.html#:~:text=the%20redefined%20service.-,Use%20case%201%3A%20Adding%20a%20new%20property%20to%20an%20existing%20entity,-Use%20case%202

entry.title: User needs only display access in EDOC_COCKPIT tcode/app in SAP S4 Hana Production.
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We received a request from the user who needs only display access in EDOC_COCKPIT tcode/app for Spain entity in SAP S4 Hana Production.

We tested the EDOC_COCKPIT tcode/app in the QS4 Test environment, and it allows all operations, including change/cancel/delete/submit. Even activity is limited with "Display".

Could you please check and let us know if there is any feasibility to restrict only display version.

Regards,

Srinu Nelli

SAP Security & GRC

entry.title: Batch number not getting copied in Return order
entry.content:
Hi everyone, I hope you're doing well.

I'm facing an issue with copying the batch number into a return order when I create it with reference to an invoice. My issue is that I'm billing only the main item, while the batch numbers in the delivery are in sub-items. Is there any way to keep my current configuration and still get the batch number in the return order?

entry.title: How to check change log for condition contract
entry.content:
Hi Experts ,

Greetings, 

I have a bsuiness case , where the customer and materials are being added in the condition contract in change mode in WCOCO_CHANGE ,when i was tried to find change log , the system is only showing the Object values and table key but not actuall which  i or business mainatined in change mode of CC #.

Ask : Is there a way to check what has been mainatined or modified in condition contract .

The change log showing the document number , that docunet number is also not showing the complete details in CDHDR/ CDPOS Table.

So, pleae guide me

Many thanks !

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Meetup Porto
entry.content: Nice photo! I'm there! It was an excellent moment!

entry.title: Enable Now Producer will not create Documents from Sims
entry.content:
Hi, For some reason Producer has stopped generating documents from Sims. Either Word docs or PDF's. When trying to create them (as normal) they appear to be created, and get the message do you want to open the document now? But when I click yes nothing opens.

I can see a doc is now showing in the Sim structure but it does not open, if I click to preview the doc a new tab opens in the browser with an error message: 

Message error code: "UE_FIle _Does_Not_Exist

Details: Cxxxxxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx

Message: There is no file

This appears to be happening for all users. In the past when I did click to open it there would be a Word pop up asking to add a label, but I have checked and this is not the issue.

Thanks 

David

entry.title: Copy data from one BTP ABAP Instance to Another BTP ABAP Instance

How can we copy the data from one BTP ABAP system to another BTP ABAP system.

Is there any way we can access SAP HANA Cloud system dedicated for BTP ABAP ?

Reference - https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/how-to-do-a-systemcopy-from-subaccount-to-subaccount/qaq-p/12362340

Muthu

entry.title: Need to create PYSM story but with selection on month
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I need to create a story where in the user should be able to select a month from input control and for the same month a previous year booked measure is to be displayed in a table. 

Is there a way to achieve this without the need to script in the backend?

entry.title: Subcontracting process return of goods - MB04
entry.content:
Hello Guys!

I have an invoice document, of a subcontracting process. The firt Invoice, the supplier sent with the wrong value of the goods, so, the cost of the product stayed wrong in the system. So, the supplier sent and aditional invoice to make the correction of the total value os the products being returned in the process.

But, in that invoice, the material is with the quantity zero, only with the Net price correct.

My question is, is it possible to enter that invoice, by MB04, as always, with the quantity zero ?!

Appreciate the help!

entry.title: Re: Accrual reversal for customer Rebate with different c...
entry.content: Hi Gauthier,

entry.title: Re: Accrual reversal for customer Rebate with different c...
entry.content:
Hi Gauthier, Thank you for your response.

Just to add, this is what happens when we do the reversal. You see the bill-to party here is indeed the same cotract owner. 

But what this is causing financial posting discrepancy, when they run the report the released accruals are posting to contract owner. Since when we do the reversal of the accruals, what we want is to release it back to customer 100000302.

entry.title: Re-execute KEU5 and KE27
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I'm new member here and need your help.

I executed KEU5 and KE27, but after that I revised some expenses. So can I do execute KEU5 and KE27 again?

Or I have to do anything else to correct it.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Integrate My Inbox link to SAP Flexible Workflow E-Mail notification
entry.content: Did a quick research on it and found this question. Apparently, sy-sysid is not available within the $session variable however it is suggested to use table TADIR for that. Mechanics should be same as the approach from this blog.

entry.title: Atualizações no Conhecimento de Transporte Eletrônico: CT-e simplificado
entry.content:
English version here 

Olá pessoal,

A equipe SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de emissão de fatura de entrada para o Brasil entregou atualizações no Conhecimento de Transporte Eletrônico (CT-e). A atualização está conforme a Nota Técnica 2024.002 versão 1.01 da SEFAZ (CT-e simplificado). Essa nova versão entrará em produção em 7 de outubro de 2024, conforme a autoridade fiscal.

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance - Nota Técnica 2024.002 versão 1.01 da SEFAZ (CT-e simplificado)

Os processos CT-e Básico e CT-e Flex suportam agora o arquivo XML de CT-e simplificado. Você pode implementar essas modificações na opção de emissão de fatura de entrada para o Brasil seguindo as instruções na SAP Note 3519421 e também na SAP Note 3519422.

Confira as últimas atualizações e recursos na sessão What's New no portal SAP Help:

  • SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP ERP

Para um passo a passo sobre como usar o SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de emissão de fatura de entrada e de faturamento de saída para o Brasil, consulte as seguintes páginas do portal SAP Help:

  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de emissão de fatura de entrada para o Brasil
  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de faturamento de saída para o Brasil

Você pode receber alerta por email quando novas SAP Notes forem lançadas ou atualizadas nos componentes listados abaixo; basta seguir as instruções do SAP Knowledge Base Article 2171560.

  • CA-GTF-CSC-EDO-BR (opção de emissão de fatura de entrada para o Brasil)
  • LOD-LH-NFE (opção de faturamento de saída para o Brasil)

Utilize o gerenciador de modificações legais para ter uma noção do que está planejado para ser suportado. Confira o SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 e a página Regulatory Change Manager para entender como usar essa ferramenta.

Gostou desse post? Dê um “like” (kudos) e compartilhe o conteúdo com seus colegas. Sinta-se à vontade para deixar suas perguntas no SAP Community e ler posts semelhantes no blogging space do SAP Document and Reporting Compliance. Para acompanhar as últimas notícias da NF-e, siga a tag SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica) e a comunidade SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.

Obrigado e até a próxima,
Thiago

Tags: brazil, brasil, einvoice, einvoicing, e-invoicing, electronic invoice, electronic invoicing, edocument, edoc, e-document, e-docs, e-doc, e-docs, drc, document compliance, nota fiscal eletrônica, electronic nota fiscal, outbound, nf-e, technical note, 2024.002, nt 2024.002, documento fiscal eletronico, electronic fiscal document, badi, dacte, badi_edoc_br_cte_simp_dacte, cl_edoc_br_dacte_s4, cl_edoc_br_dacte_op

entry.title: Re: End-to-End Business Processes for the Intelligent Enterprise
entry.content: Are there any good online practice exams available?  How is everyone here preparing for the exam?

entry.title: Sap Webi Multivalue Error
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have multiple queries, and I linked all queries with the main one by ID
but I have duplicates when merge it into one sheet.

Any solution for this issue?

 

Regards.

entry.title: Re: Error in creation material master MM01
entry.content: Look at the received data for wrong values (in debug double-click on the it_marc... internal tables)

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Dear @David_Chaviano

I am a woman  my name is Nada and I'm excited to be part of the SAP Community Beginner Corner! I work for an organization called Edraky,
which is focused on providing comprehensive SAP training and implementation services to businesses across the Middle East. I'm writing to apply for certification  Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public EditionC_S4CPB.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Import data for consolidation - products Error in file upload
entry.content: Hi Ramesh, Do the entries in the file contain a SOURCE_ID or material number associated with them? This error occurs when neither of those fields are populated in the file

entry.title: Automatically pack multiple materials into one handling unit (Outbound Delivery)(IM)
entry.content:
Is there a way to automatically pack multiple materials (items) into one handling unit (HU) in Inventory Management (IM)? From what I understand, the Automatic Packing process in an outbound delivery (POP/POF) only allows one item per delivery to be automatically packed into one HU/packaging Material

I have two cases:

1. Different materials that should be automatically packed into one handling unit .

2. The same material, listed as multiple items, that should be packed into one HU.

which is focused on providing comprehensive SAP training and implementation services to businesses across the Middle East. I'm writing to apply for certification  Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public EditionC_S4CPB.

Thanks

entry.title: "Dynamic" Email Body for Email Notification in SAP BTP Workflow or How to add a table dynamically
entry.content:
Intro

In this blog, we will present a simple, easy solution to address the issue of creating a dynamic email body and displaying multiple rows of a table in your static email notification.

We all know that we are moving to SAP Build Process Automation, but I want to address an issue that has happened to me multiple times in the Workflow in Business Application Studio. I recently discovered a workaround that can be useful to many developers.

We are talking about all the emails we send through the Workflow using the Mail Task. These emails usually refer to a static HTML file with elements from our context bound statically in the HTML. Example:

 

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
</head>
<body>
    <p><i>Simple example to use the content in the html.</i></p>

    <div class="table-container">
        <h4 class="section-title">TIME ENTRY REJECTION</h4>
        <p>You have <b>${context._week_info_to}</b> for Project <b>${context._project_id} – ${context._project_name}</b> that was rejected by <b>${context._wp_pm_name} (${context._wp_pm_id})</b> with the following comments (if any):</p>
        </div>
</body>
</html>

 

In our example, the use case is the following: a manager rejects a number of an employee's activities per week, and we want to send that groups all the rejected activities in a table within the email. We do not know how many activities there will be; it could be 1, 2, or 7. We want a nice and clean table that shows only the rejected rows, with no borders or fake information from others.

How can we achieve this?

Activities

  1. We need to discuss a limit with the stakeholders. In our case, it’s the maximum number of activities a manager can reject for that employee in a single week. We set the maximum at 10 (in your case, it could be 100 or 1,000)—it's the maximum number of rows in your table.

  2. Modify the HTML code to include all the lines up to the limit we set before, paying special attention to the email's CSS.

  3. Develop a good JS script task to prepare our email context and set the correct CSS attributes.

Limit of elements, the starting point

To create truly dynamic content in an email, we would typically use JavaScript. However, this is generally considered a bad idea due to security concerns, and it's not a feature that's commonly available, especially given SAP's strong focus on security. Instead, we use a semi-dynamic approach.

This involves adding as many rows as possible to the table, safe in the knowledge that HTML doesn't produce errors if something is undefined. In these cases, we will simply have an empty space.

To implement this, we must clarify the table's limits with the stakeholders. If necessary, we can include a 100% buffer.

In our example scenario, the limit is 10 rejected requests, so I will set it to 20.

The HTML

As I said before the html will include all the possible lines + buffer. And we will also consider in our lines the css value that permit to hide and show the row that we are not going to show.

 

 

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Time Rejection Notification</title>
    <style>
        body {
            font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
            margin: 0;
            padding: 20px;
            background-color: #f4f4f9;
        }
        h4, h3 {
            color: #333;
            margin-bottom: 10px;
        }
        p {
            color: #555;
            line-height: 1.6;
            margin-bottom: 10px;
        }
        b {
            color: #333;
        }
        .section-title {
            border-bottom: 2px solid #333;
            padding-bottom: 5px;
            margin-bottom: 10px;
        }
        .table-container {
            margin-bottom: 20px;
            padding: 10px;
            background-color: #fff;
            border: 1px solid #ddd;
            border-radius: 5px;
        }
        table {
            width: 100%;
            border-collapse: collapse;
            margin-top: 20px;
        }
        th, td {
            border: 1px solid #dddddd;
            text-align: left;
            padding: 8px;
        }
        th {
            background-color: #f2f2f2;
        }
        .hidden-row {
            display: none;
        }
        .footer {
            font-size: 0.8em;
            color: #888;
            margin-top: 20px;
        }
    </style>
</head>
<body>
    <p><i>Please review the rejected time entries below and resubmit as necessary.</i></p>

    <div class="table-container">
        <h4 class="section-title">TIME ENTRY REJECTION</h4>
        <p>You have time for the period ending <b>${context._week_info_to}</b> for Project <b>${context._project_id} – ${context._project_name}</b> that was rejected by <b>${context._wp_pm_name} (${context._wp_pm_id})</b> with the following comments (if any):</p>

        <table border="1">
            <thead>
                <tr>
                    <th>Week Ending</th>
                    <th>Project ID</th>
                    <th>Project Description</th>
                    <th>WP Name</th>
                    <th>Prop Hours</th>
                    <th>Activity Type Desc</th>
                    <th>Note</th>
                </tr>
            </thead>
            <tbody>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[0].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[0].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[0]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[0]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[0]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[0]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[0]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[0]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[1].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[1].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[1]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[1]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[1]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[1]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[1]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[1]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[2].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[2].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[2]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[2]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[2]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[2]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[2]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[2]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[3].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[3].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[3]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[3]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[3]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[3]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[3]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[3]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[4].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[4].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[4]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[4]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[4]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[4]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[4]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[4]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[5].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[5].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[5]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[5]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[5]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[5]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[5]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[5]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[6].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[6].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[6]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[6]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[6]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[6]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[6]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[6]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[7].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[7].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[7]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[7]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[7]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[7]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[7]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[7]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[8].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[8].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[8]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[8]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[8]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[8]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[8]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[8]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[9].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[9].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[9]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[9]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[9]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[9]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[9]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[9]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[10].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[10].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[10]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[10]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[10]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[10]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[10]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[10]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[11].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[11].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[11]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[11]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[11]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[11]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[11]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[11]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[12].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[12].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[12]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[12]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[12]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[12]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[12]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[12]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[13].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[13].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[13]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[13]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[13]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[13]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[13]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[13]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[14].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[14].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[14]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[14]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[14]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[14]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[14]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[14]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[15].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[15].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[15]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[15]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[15]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[15]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[15]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[15]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[16].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[16].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[16]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[16]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[16]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[16]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[16]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[16]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[17].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[17].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[17]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[17]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[17]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[17]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[17]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[17]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[18].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[18].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[18]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[18]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[18]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[18]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[18]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[18]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[19].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[19].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[19]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[19]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[19]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[19]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[19]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[19]._note}</td>
                </tr>
            </tbody>
        </table>  

        <p>“Please submit time only for the time that has been rejected from the app <a href=${context.link} target="_blank">My Inbox</a>. Reach out to your Work Package manager with any questions. Thank you.”</p>
    </div>

    
</body>
</html>

 

You see that each line of the table <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[count].class} > has ready the attribute class, and it's dynamically bonded with the maximum number of rows we decided at the beginning of the previous step. You can also notice that I added as a CSS class definition the following CSS code: 

.hidden-row {
            display: none;
        }

This code will simple help to hide the row we are not using, we will use this class in the next session. 

JS Script

Now, we need to create our JS code. We set this code usually in the script task right before sending the email:
Script Task

var entries = $.context._time_entries; //Array of what we want to send in the email

var rejectedEntries = [];
for (count = 0; count < 20; count++) {
	if(count >= entries.length){
		rejectedEntries.push({ class : "hidden-row" });
	} else {
		entries[count].class = "";
		rejectedEntries.push(entries[count]);
	}
}

$.context._time_entries = rejectedEntries;

This is a simple loop, but it contains a hidden logic.
During each iteration, we increment the index variable count regardless of whether our array of entries has ended or not. We continue this loop until we reach the maximum number of rows we specified at the beginning in this blog. For example, if we have 4 valid entries to write in our table ( entries.length = 4 ), for all those four entries, we set the class property equal to empty (line 8). When our count points to an undefined element (ideally when our index is greater than the length of the array), we set the class property for that row to "hidden-row".
After this procedure, we can imagine our JSON array to be similar to this:

[
    {
        "class": "",
        "z_date": "2024-10-01",
        "_project_id": "101",
        "_project_name": "Project Sunrise",
        "_wp_name": "Initial Planning",
        "_prop_hours": "4",
        "_activity_type_desc": "Planning",
        "_note": "Kick-off meeting with the client"
    },
    {
        "class": "",
        "z_date": "2024-10-02",
        "_project_id": "102",
        "_project_name": "Project Apollo",
        "_wp_name": "Requirements Gathering",
        "_prop_hours": "6",
        "_activity_type_desc": "Analysis",
        "_note": "Requirement documentation"
    },
    {
        "class": "",
        "z_date": "2024-10-03",
        "_project_id": "103",
        "_project_name": "Project Legacy",
        "_wp_name": "Design",
        "_prop_hours": "3",
        "_activity_type_desc": "Design",
        "_note": "Initial design sketches"
    },
    {
        "class": "",
        "z_date": "2024-10-04",
        "_project_id": "104",
        "_project_name": "Project Titan",
        "_wp_name": "Development",
        "_prop_hours": "5",
        "_activity_type_desc": "Coding",
        "_note": "Development of core modules"
    },
    {"class": "hidden-row"},
    {"class": "hidden-row"},
    {"class": "hidden-row"},
    {"class": "hidden-row"},
    {"class": "hidden-row"},
    {"class": "hidden-row"},
    {"class": "hidden-row"},
    {"class": "hidden-row"},
    {"class": "hidden-row"},
    {"class": "hidden-row"},
    {"class": "hidden-row"},
    {"class": "hidden-row"},
    {"class": "hidden-row"},
    {"class": "hidden-row"},
    {"class": "hidden-row"},
    {"class": "hidden-row"}
]

Now you can connect all the dots: if you send the email with this structure, the rows after the first 4 will be hidden automatically without creating an unsightly and unnecessary empty row that could disrupt the visual appearance of your email.

Conclusion

By integrating HTML and JavaScript in SAP Build Process Automation ( Workflow in BAS), you can effectively create dynamic, visually appealing email notifications that adapt to varying amounts of data. Whether you're notifying employees of time entry rejections or any other updates, this approach provides a "flexible" solution for your HTML email. Even if in the future we will use low-code/no-code

entry.title: Re: AI Assisted Skills Architecture Creation
entry.content:
HI @MartinPosinger,  @BrandanHartland ,

Currently, our charging mechanism is based on the number of Job Profiles linked to roles that undergo AI-assisted skill extraction. For each Job Profile that is successfully mapped with AI-extracted skills, we charge 2 AI units.

For more uptodate information about AI charges, please reach out to your dedicated account team. They can provide you with detailed pricing details.

Best Regards,

Sharath

entry.title: Re: Standard Workflows in Production Planning and Quality...
entry.content:
Hi Dominik ,

Thank you reply .

i already gone through this link but i didnt find any workflow for process order and Quality management.

entry.title: Re: Load 0WBS into internal table in start routine to loo...
entry.content:
The internal format for WBS element is 8 numeric characters, and external format is 24 alphanumeric characters. This is usually handled by a conversion-exit.

  • Those are copied in BW into fields WBS_ELEMT and WBS_ELM_EX. WBS_ELM_EX
  • OBJNR is a 22 alphanumeric character  field, for WBS its value is 'PR' concatenated with the 8 numeric character field and trailing spaces.

You could fill the internal table used in FOR ALL ENTRIES with only co_objnr+2(8)

entry.title: Re: Automatically pack multiple materials into one handli...
entry.content:
Hello @DCRG 

Yes, that's perfectly possible. If you build a packing specification that packs e.g. 5 PC of the material into one HU, then even if you have 5 delivery items of 1 PC each, all of them will be packed into a single HU.

If packing specifications don't work for you, you can build your own automatic packing logic with the BADI_HU_AUTOPACK enhancement.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: I am trying to create a Date Range to get a total of a measure, here from_date is an user input
entry.content: Read the documentation. https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_latest_index_htm/latest/en-US/index.htm?file=abencds_date_functions_v2.htm

entry.title: Re: jConnect version for HA functionality
entry.content:
jConnect 16.0 from SDK 16.0 SP03 and higher will support HADR (Always On) operations as documented at SAP jConnect Features for HADR.

My recommendation would be to use the jConnect version matching the base ASE release, or higher.  If you use a higher jConnect release, remember to install the corresponding sp's - remember, jConnect is two parts - thin java driver AND sp installation).

If you have jconn42.jar available, use that .jar, not jconn4.jar for JDBC 4.2 support and ongoing development (only jconn42.jar will be released in future SDK and ASE releases).  The Programmers Reference gives examples of how to use this (it's only a change to the class being called).

Chris

entry.title: Re: How it was SAP Inside Track Porto 2024
entry.content:
Hi ,

Thanks to everyone involved in making the SAP Inside Track event in Porto a success!

Regards ,

entry.title: Re: MRP X MRP live
entry.content: These are only technical differences, however MRP Live differs from the classic one also functionally.

entry.title: Re: Error in SAP Build apps Build service 4.11.167
entry.content: SAP must have fixed this because everything works now! I did not do anything.

entry.title: Re: Detailed steps to establish integration between Azure blob Storage & BTP CPI
entry.content:
Hi Rohini,

Thanks for the detailed overview! The step-by-step guide on setting up Azure storage and integrating it with CPI is very helpful. I appreciate the clarity you've provided, especially with the testing process. Looking forward to implementing this!

Regards ,

entry.title: Re: MRP X MRP live
entry.content:
Hello @ADILSONBENNY 

Consider the following notes on the differences between MRP Live and classic MRP:

  • 2023766 - MRP Live/MRP Classic: Performance Information
  • 2640393 - Differences between transaction MD01N and classic MRP transactions MD01/MD02/MD03
  • 1914010 - MD01N: Restrictions for Planning in MRP Live on HANA
  • 2638465 - MRP differences between S/4HANA and ERP systems

Also consider the following blog posts:

  • S/4HANA MRP - to Live or not to Live: MRP lists
  • Integration issues in S/4HANA – Evaluation of MRP planning results
  • S/4HANA MRP – to Live or not to Live: MRP areas of responsibility
  • S/4HANA MRP – to Live or not to Live: Planning scope

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Text Association in DWC not getting reflected in SAC...
entry.content:
I also faced this issue more than once now. Always there is description available post association in Datasphere Analytic model however Story will not budge; it will show ID (key) and description (key) 🤔

Its due to data access language on SAC which is set to other than what I have used in Datasphere. Mostly its English but for testing I change to "DE", "ES" etc. But it you also do it more often then please make sure your language key (if its "E" in Datasphere) should be same to Data access language as SAC (English) as shown in below snip. 

#firstanswer #community_contribution

entry.title: Re: Standard Workflows in Production Planning and Quality...
entry.content: That's all what SAP delivers in standard. You can always implement your own scenarios.

entry.title: Re: Can you remember the first time you heard of SAP
entry.content: Currently I am working with SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics cloud. Looking forward to SAP TechED; thanks for sharing.

Hi, I'd like to ask if this link/view is brand new in SAP4Me or already available?

Thank you

Marcel

entry.title: NET INVOICE AMOUNT field (NET_INV_AMOUNT) missing in sap tm table /scmtms/d_sf_rot
entry.content:
Hi Team,

NET INVOICE AMOUNT field (NET_INV_AMOUNT) missing in sap tm table /scmtms/d_sf_rot

I could see the same field in other clients but not with my client any reasons for this?


Thanks

entry.title: Re: Asttx field not updateing in va42
entry.content:
Did you get an error when saving (check with SM13 or even SLG1)?

  • Also, RV45A-ASTTX is linked to the user status, shouldn't these changes be reflected in the RV45A-STTXT system status - User status are defined in your system, when sytem ones are defined by SAP, so you must check locally

entry.title: Why is a SAP Build APPS app 10 times faster on an Android device then on a web server?
entry.content: I have built and app and tested it both on a webbserver and as an app on an Android device. When I run it on the tablet it is approx 10 times faster then when I run the app on the webbserver. I tested it on the local webbserver. It is also slow when I launch the app in the preview mode in SAP Build apps. Does anyone know if I can do something to fix this? Thanks?

entry.title: Re: Error in SAP Build apps Build service 4.11.167
entry.content:
Hello

I received from SAP an answer 2 days ago:

 

And all is fine now

entry.title: It is possible to make a 'https' API call in ABAP?
entry.content: I'm tried to call to a 'https' API, and i only do it to a 'http' insecure call of the same API. It is possible to make a 'https' API call in ABAP?

entry.title: Re: requires a valid tax code good receipt
entry.content:
'goods receipt based on a purchase order' seems to be a wrong assertion...

  • Which transaction/application do you use, where did you input the PO number?

entry.title: Learning
entry.content: Hi all, I am currently an intern specializing in SAP MM, and I would like to express my gratitude for the Digital Skills Initiative. I am highly motivated to participate because I believe that enhancing my digital skills will greatly complement my current experience and help me grow in my professional journey. This opportunity aligns perfectly with my career goals, and I look forward to learning and contributing actively. Thank you for this fantastic initiative!
